data.title: Roche, Prothena miss main goal in mid-stage trial for Parkinson’s drug
data.description:
Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) announced Thursday that prasinezumab, its experimental therapy developed with Prothena Corporation (NASDAQ:PRTA) for Parkinson’s disease, missed the primary endpoint in a mid-stage trial.

The Swiss pharma giant said that while its Phase IIb PADOVA trial for early PD patients demonstrated numerical clinical efficacy for the monoclonal antibody, the effects lacked statistical significance.

According to the company, the PADOVA trial missed the primary goal of time to confirmed motor progression, evaluated using a clinical measure called the Movement Disorder Society-Unified Parkinson’s Disease Rating Scale [MDS-UPDRS].

While prasinezumab led to a numerical delay in motor progression without statistical significance, the effect was even more prominent in those who have undergone FDA-approved PD therapy, levodopa.

PADOVA enrolled more than 500 patients with early-stage PD, including 75% of participants who had received prior levodopa therapy. The experimental drug was well tolerated, and there were no new safety signals.

Roche (OTCQX:RHHBF) said it would continue to advance its Phase II PASADENA and Phase IIb PADOVA studies to investigate the observed clinical effect of prasinezumab.

“The results from the Phase 2b PADOVA study are a significant step forward to potentially bring the first disease-modifying treatment option to the millions of individuals living with Parkinson’s disease and their families,” noted Prothena (NASDAQ:PRTA) CEO Gene Kinney. Shares of the Irish biotech added ~9% in the premarket despite the setback.

data.title: U.S. GDP revised up to 3.1% in third estimate
data.description:
• The Commerce Department's third, and final, estimate of U.S. GDP in Q3 2024 settled at +3.1% Q/Q from its previous estimate of 2.8%, the agency said on Thursday.
  • Breaking news... check back for updates.

data.title: Visa announces completion of Featurespace acquisition
data.description:
• Visa (NYSE:V) (NEOE:VISA:CA) on Thursday announced the completion of the acquisition of the AI payments protection technology developer, Featurespace.
  • V shares were trading +0.74% Thursday pre-market at $312.07.
  • The digital payments company had first announced the acquisition in September.
  • Featurespace is set to become a part of Visa's Risk and Identity Solutions business unit.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Fed induced tech sell-off is just a 'buying opportunity,' Wedbush
data.description:
Wednesday's sharp sell-off in the stock market induced by a "less dovish" Federal Reserve is nothing more than a "buying opportunity," investment firm Wedbush Securities said.

"As we have hand held investors the last few years on this Fed path, this sell-off is just another buying opportunity to own tech winners poised to play in a robust AI Revolution into 2025," analysts at the firm wrote in a note to clients. "We will see more of these bumpy days (Fed, Trump tariff fears, etc.) as the bears come out of hibernation after missing a historic tech rally....but ultimately it does not move the needle for a soft landing and bullish backdrop for risk-on assets."

SoftBank's big bet

The firm has outlined three "key positives" for big tech companies looking into 2025, including the announcement by SoftBank's (OTCPK:SFTBY) founder Masayoshi Son that the company will invest $100B in the U.S. over the next four years, due in part to the upcoming Trump administration.

The expectation is that there will be "significant" artificial intelligence initiatives from Washington next year, which should benefit companies like Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and other tech companies, the analysts said. AI should also be a major part in Department of Defense spending, which could be a "major tailwind" for companies like Palantir (PLTR) and Oracle (ORCL), the analysts added.

"We believe the Softbank $100 billion AI announcement in Mar-a-Lago by CEO Masayoshi Son is just the beginning of more global AI investments in the U.S. which is a bullish backdrop for the broader tech and AI landscape," the analysts wrote.

Khan gone

Another positive is the departure of Lina Khan at the Federal Trade Commission, as Khan will be replaced by Andrew Ferguson. And while Ferguson will have a "keen eye" on the tech world, he is expected to roll back much of Khan's "head scratching anti-tech agenda."

"In essence, Christmas came early for the tech world as the Khan overhang is removed for the tech world at a key time in the AI arms race in which we expect the strong to get stronger as Mag 7 gets the engines started up again on M&A with Microsoft, Oracle, Alphabet, Amazon, and Tesla set to accelerate deals to expand their technology moat," the analysts wrote.

Musk and Trump

And last but not least, Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and Elon Musk are expected to benefit, given Musk's close relationship with President-elect Trump.

Trump is likely to accelerate the timeline for Tesla's full self-driving and autonomous driving initiatives, the analysts said.

"We believe a Trump White House helps unlock the $1 trillion of autonomous/AI value to Tesla's stock as autonomous/FSD is accelerated starting in 2025 and a key tailwind for Cybercab timing," the analysts wrote.

data.title: Leidos wins $987M contract to support F-16 Foreign Military Sales sustainment
data.description:
Leidos (NYSE:LDOS) has been awarded a contract to provide critical engineering and sustainment services for Foreign Military Sales customers of the U.S. Air Force's F-16 Fighting Falcon.

The F-16 has been procured by over 25 nations around the world, supporting a global fleet size of approximately 2,200 active aircraft.

The contract was awarded by the Air Force Life Cycle Management Center, supporting the F-16 System Program Office. Under the contract, Leidos will provide post-production support through critical engineering and sustainment services necessary for the continued operation and improvement of the F-16 weapon system.

The single award, indefinite delivery, indefinite quantity contract has a maximum value of $987M and 10-year period of performance.

data.title: Stock index futures rise a day after Fed-fueled selloff
data.description:
Stock index futures extended their gains on Thursday, a day after Wall Street tumbled as the Federal Reserve projected fewer rate cuts in 2025.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.8%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.8% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.5%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.53%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 3 basis points to 4.33%.

U.S. stocks on Wednesday slumped after the Federal Reserve projected higher inflation and fewer rate cuts in 2025. The estimates came after the central bank announced a quarter-point rate cut, as widely expected.

"There might only be six days until Christmas, but markets still had time for another surprise yesterday, as a hawkish cut from the Fed saw the S&P 500 post its biggest decline after a Fed meeting since 2001," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

While the Fed delivered another 25 basis point rate cut on Wednesday, the overall messaging from the central bank was a much slower pace of policy easing in 2025.

"The moves led to a significant cross-asset slump, with the 10 year Treasury yield closing above 4.5% for the first time since May, whilst the VIX index of volatility surged, and touched its highest since the market turmoil back in the summer," Allen added.

The Fed cut rates as expected, but hawkish forecasts and press conference language surprised markets, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

On the economic calendar for Thursday, the third estimate of the Q3 GDP will land before the opening bell.

The initial jobless claims will also be release at the same time, and is expected to fall to 229K.

The November existing home sales and November leading indicators are also slated to land during market hours. The former is expected to rise to 4.09M, while the latter is forecasted to come in at -0.1% M/M.

The Fed Balance Sheet will be released later in the day.

data.title: PyroGenesis announces debt-to-equity conversion of client receivable
data.description:
• PyroGenesis (TSX:PYR:CA) has announced an agreement with HPQ Silicon (HPQ:CA) to resolve an outstanding liability of $4.94M owed to PyroGenesis, using a debt-to-equity conversion.
  • Under the agreement, the outstanding liability will be converted into 17.97M units at a price of $0.275/unit.
  • Each unit will be comprised of one common share of HPQ and one common share price warrant.
  • Each warrant will entitle PyroGenesis to acquire one common share of HPQ at a price of $0.285 until the date that is two years following the effective date of this transaction.
  • Upon issuance of the units to PyroGenesis, the outstanding liability shall be deemed fully satisfied.
